payload.text:
ROLE & MISSION

You are designing a premium, next-generation learning app that teaches Specialized Legislation for Audiovisual Communication students in the most efficient, cognitively optimized way possible.

This is NOT a traditional study app.
This is a cognitive performance system designed to:
	•	Eliminate passive learning
	•	Prevent boredom
	•	Maximize retention
	•	Develop analytical thinking
	•	Train legal reasoning
	•	Prepare for real case application
	•	Optimize performance for multiple-choice exams AND practical case studies

The app must feel intellectually elite, but cognitively simple.

⸻

CORE STRUCTURE

The app has 3 progressive levels:

⸻

LEVEL 1 — “DUMMIES FOR LEGISLATION”

Objective:

Make the student understand complex legal concepts in the simplest possible way.

Rules:
	•	No legal jargon unless absolutely necessary
	•	Everything explained through analogies
	•	Use storytelling
	•	Use real-life media examples (TV, YouTube, Netflix, influencers, ads)
	•	Micro-lessons (3–5 minutes max)
	•	One concept per screen

Teaching Style:
	•	“Explain like I’m 15 but don’t make me feel stupid.”
	•	Use metaphors (e.g., Freedom of Expression = oxygen of democracy)
	•	Visual logic trees
	•	“If this… then that…” reasoning

After each micro-lesson:
	1.	3 quick recall questions
	2.	1 applied mini-scenario
	3.	1 “Explain it back in your own words” prompt

Add:
	•	AI detects weak areas
	•	Immediate correction with explanation
	•	Confidence meter

⸻

LEVEL 2 — “SMARTIES FOR LEGISLATION”

Objective:

Develop structured legal reasoning.

Now we move from understanding → analyzing.

Features:
	•	Case-based learning
	•	Realistic scenarios from:
	•	Advertising regulation
	•	Copyright in digital platforms
	•	Freedom of expression conflicts
	•	Radio/TV regulation
	•	Internet law

Learning Method:
	•	Socratic questioning
	•	No instant answers
	•	AI asks:
	•	What rights are involved?
	•	Who are the subjects?
	•	Is there a conflict?
	•	What legal principle applies?
	•	What is the limit?

Structure per topic:
	1.	Concept refresher (compressed)
	2.	Case simulation
	3.	Guided reasoning steps
	4.	Student writes legal reasoning
	5.	AI evaluates logic structure, not just correctness

Add:
	•	Argument quality score
	•	Logical coherence score
	•	“Professional reasoning improvement suggestions”

⸻

LEVEL 3 — “WISE FOR LEGISLATION”

Objective:

Think like a media lawyer.

Now we train:
	•	Decision-making
	•	Ethical evaluation
	•	Risk assessment
	•	Legal strategy

Advanced Features:

1.⁠ ⁠Multi-layered case simulations
Cases evolve based on student decisions.

Example:
A TV channel broadcasts controversial content.
→ Public backlash
→ Regulatory investigation
→ Civil lawsuit
→ Constitutional claim

Student must:
	•	Identify legal risks
	•	Choose strategy
	•	Anticipate consequences

AI simulates outcomes.

⸻

2.⁠ ⁠Conflict Labs
Simulated clashes:
	•	Freedom of expression vs honor
	•	Advertising vs consumer protection
	•	Copyright vs digital sharing
	•	Regulation vs innovation

Student must justify position like in a court.

⸻

3.⁠ ⁠Exam Optimization Mode
Since final exam = multiple choice (20 questions, no penalty for wrong answers):

Include:
	•	Time-pressure simulations
	•	Pattern-recognition training
	•	Trap-answer detection training
	•	Cognitive bias awareness
	•	“Why this wrong answer is tempting” analysis

⸻

DIFFERENTIATION FROM NORMAL STUDY APPS

This app must:

✔ Use Active Recall
✔ Use Spaced Repetition
✔ Use Interleaving (mix topics strategically)
✔ Track cognitive fatigue
✔ Adapt difficulty dynamically
✔ Force output before input
✔ Train reasoning, not memorization

⸻

PERSONALIZATION ENGINE

The AI must:
	•	Detect weak competencies (e.g., conflict analysis, subject identification)
	•	Adapt future cases accordingly
	•	Track improvement curve
	•	Suggest when to review old material
	•	Identify overconfidence

⸻

UI/UX PHILOSOPHY
	•	Minimalist
	•	Premium dark mode option
	•	No childish gamification
	•	Instead: intellectual progression indicators
	•	“From Student → Analyst → Strategist” evolution badge

⸻

PEDAGOGICAL FOUNDATION

The system must integrate:
	•	Bloom’s Taxonomy progression
	•	Retrieval-based learning
	•	Desirable difficulties
	•	Metacognition prompts
	•	Reflective learning
	•	Case method
	•	Problem-based learning
	•	Cooperative simulation mode (optional multiplayer debate mode)

⸻

CONTENT STRUCTURE (Based on Course Units)

The app must structure content around:
	1.	Introduction to Communication Law
	2.	Fundamental Rights and Communication
	3.	Advertising Legal Regime
	4.	Copyright and Digital Economy
	5.	Regulation of Radio, TV, Cinema and Internet

Each unit must follow:

Dummies → Smarties → Wise progression.

⸻

ADDITIONAL PREMIUM FEATURES

1.⁠ ⁠“Explain to a Non-Lawyer” Mode

User must explain concept to:
	•	A YouTuber
	•	A TV producer
	•	An Influencer
	•	A Journalist

AI rates clarity.

⸻

2.⁠ ⁠“Legal Red Flag Detector”

AI presents scenarios.
User must detect hidden legal violations.

⸻

3.⁠ ⁠“Build Your Own Media Company”

User makes decisions:
	•	Advertising model
	•	Content strategy
	•	Copyright handling
	•	Risk management

AI simulates regulatory impact.

⸻

EMOTIONAL DESIGN

The app must:
	•	Avoid cognitive overload
	•	Avoid boredom
	•	Avoid long paragraphs
	•	Encourage intellectual confidence
	•	Make the user feel sharper after each session

⸻

OUTPUT FORMAT

When building content:
	•	Short structured blocks
	•	Clear hierarchy
	•	No fluff
	•	No over-explaining
	•	Precision + clarity

⸻

FINAL INSTRUCTION TO GEMINI

Design the full app architecture including:
	•	Feature structure
	•	Learning flow
	•	Adaptive logic
	•	User journey
	•	Example lesson from each level
	•	Example case simulation
	•	Exam training sample
	•	AI feedback example
	•	Monetization model
	•	Future scalability

This must feel like a next-generation cognitive training system for law mastery.

⸻

Eğer istersen sana şimdi:
	•	💎 Daha da premium “investor pitch version”
	•	🧠 Sadece cognitive science odaklı versiyon
	•	🎯 Sadece exam-killer optimized versiyon
	•	📱 UI wireframe mantığında versiyon

**Defining the Vision**

I am currently focused on defining the core philosophy. I believe it should center on cognitive optimization, emphasizing active recall and spaced repetition for elite learning. I am picturing a premium, next-generation app for "Specialized Legislation for Audiovisual Communication" students, tailored for their specific needs.


**Refining the App Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the app's core features. I've designed a three-tiered progression system to scaffold learning and considered specific content units for the "Specialized Legislation for Audiovisual Communication" focus. I have also developed specific key features for the application, like AI-driven personalization and multi-layered case simulations, along with tech specs for the core stack. I've also begun to establish the essential file structure.


**Detailing the Interface**

I am now structuring the UI components. I've designed `LevelCard.tsx` for level navigation and `ContentStage.tsx` for the learning interface. I'm focusing on simplicity for Level 1, using prompt engineering for clarity. Level 2 incorporates a Socratic method, and Level 3 features a multi-step simulation. The design prioritizes dark mode, high contrast, sophisticated typography, and professional reasoning badges. I've decided Unit 1 will be an "Introduction to Law".

entries.description: Primary entry point served in the browser
Primary entry point of the application
Active learning interface for Level 1, 2, and 3
